title: Ensure EC2 instances use IMDSv2
description:
EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) allows EC2 instances to access metadata about themselves. IMDSv2 is a more secure session-oriented method that requires a session token, preventing SSRF-based metadata theft.

Status: EC2 instance i-0abc123def456789 does not require IMDSv2.

Risk: Without IMDSv2, an SSRF vulnerability in an application running on EC2 could allow an attacker to steal IAM credentials from the metadata service.

payload.remediation: Require IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances by setting HttpTokens to 'required' in instance metadata options.
payload.status_extended: EC2 instance i-0abc123def456789 does not require IMDSv2.
payload.risk: Without IMDSv2, an SSRF vulnerability in an application running on EC2 could allow an attacker to steal IAM credentials from the metadata service.

raw.StatusExtended: EC2 instance i-0abc123def456789 does not require IMDSv2.
raw.Description: EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) allows EC2 instances to access metadata about themselves. IMDSv2 is a more secure session-oriented method that requires a session token, preventing SSRF-based metadata theft.
raw.Risk: Without IMDSv2, an SSRF vulnerability in an application running on EC2 could allow an attacker to steal IAM credentials from the metadata service.

Code.CLI: aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id i-0abc123def456789 --http-tokens required --http-endpoint enabled

Recommendation.Text: Require IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances by setting HttpTokens to 'required' in instance metadata options.
